gaudapada/mandukya-karika/3.15.english_close: The creation-teaching that has been stated variously through clay, metal, sparks, and the like, that is a pedagogical means for bringing one down into understanding; there is no difference whatsoever.

gaudapada/mandukya-karika/3.16.sanskrit_iast:
āśramās trividhā hīnamadhyamotkṛṣṭadṛṣṭayaḥ
upāsanopadiṣṭeyaṃ tadartham anukampayā
gaudapada/mandukya-karika/3.16.english_close: The stages and viewpoints are threefold: inferior, middling, and superior. This *upāsanā* has been taught for their sake, out of compassion.

gaudapada/mandukya-karika/3.17.sanskrit_iast:
svasiddhāntavyavasthāsu dvaitino niścitā dṛḍham
parasparaṃ virudhyante tair ayaṃ na virudhyate
gaudapada/mandukya-karika/3.17.english_close: Dualists stand firmly fixed in the arrangements of their own doctrines. They contradict one another; but by them this teaching is not contradicted.

gaudapada/mandukya-karika/3.18.sanskrit_iast:
advaitaṃ paramārtho hi dvaitaṃ tadbheda ucyate
teṣām ubhayathā dvaitaṃ tenāyaṃ na viruddhyate
gaudapada/mandukya-karika/3.18.english_close: Non-duality, indeed, is the ultimate reality; duality is said to be its differentiation. For them, duality prevails in both ways; therefore this teaching is not contradicted by them.

gaudapada/mandukya-karika/3.19.sanskrit_iast:
māyayā bhidyate hy etan nānyathājaṃ kathaṃcana
tattvato bhidyamāne hi martyatām amṛtaṃ vrajet
gaudapada/mandukya-karika/3.19.english_close: This unborn is indeed divided only by *māyā*, and in no other way whatsoever. For if it were divided in reality, the immortal would pass into mortality.

gaudapada/mandukya-karika/3.20.sanskrit_iast:
ajātasyaiva bhāvasya jātim icchanti vādinaḥ
ajāto hy amṛto bhāvo martyatāṃ katham eṣyati
gaudapada/mandukya-karika/3.20.english_close: The disputants want birth for precisely that reality which is unborn. But an unborn reality is immortal; how could it go to mortality?

gaudapada/mandukya-karika/3.21.sanskrit_iast:
na bhavaty amṛtaṃ martyaṃ na martyam amṛtaṃ tathā
prakṛter anyathābhāvo na kathaṃcid bhaviṣyati
gaudapada/mandukya-karika/3.21.english_close: The immortal does not become mortal; nor likewise does the mortal become immortal. A becoming-other than one's own nature will in no way occur.

jiva-gosvami/sat-sandarbha/§42.sanskrit_iast: tasmāt pariccheda-pratibimbatvādi-pratipādaka-śāstrāṇy api kathañcit tat-sādṛśyena gauṇyā eva vṛttyā pravarteran; ambuvad agrahaṇāt tu na tathātvam, vṛddhi-hrāsa-bhāktvam antarbhāvād ubhaya-sāmañjasyād evam, iti pūrvottara-pakṣamaya-nyāyābhyām.
jiva-gosvami/sat-sandarbha/§42.english_close: Therefore, even the scriptural texts that teach delimitation, reflection, and the like would function only by a secondary signifying force, and that only through a certain resemblance to the case at hand. “But not suchness, because it is not apprehended, as in the case of water”; “there is figurative attribution of increase and decrease because of inclusion; thus, because both sides are rendered coherent”: so runs the reasoning of the paired prior-and-subsequent arguments.

jiva-gosvami/sat-sandarbha/§43.sanskrit_iast: tata eva abheda-śāstrāṇi ubhayoś cid-rūpatve jīva-samūhasya durghaṭa-ghaṭanā-paṭīyasyā svābhāvika-tad-acintya-śaktyā svabhāvata eva tad-raśmi-paramāṇu-guṇa-sthānīyatvāt tad-vyatirekeṇa avyatirekeṇa ca virodhaṃ parihṛtya agre muhur api tad etad vyāsa-samādhi-labdha-siddhānta-yojanāya yojanīyāni.
jiva-gosvami/sat-sandarbha/§43.english_close: For that very reason, the *abheda-śāstras* too, insofar as both are of the nature of consciousness, and insofar as the aggregate of *jīvas* stands, by his natural and inconceivable power, in the position of the quality of atomic rays belonging to him, must first remove the contradiction by means of difference from him and non-difference from him. Thereafter they are repeatedly to be applied in what follows, for the sake of coordinating this doctrine received in Vyāsa’s *samādhi*.
jiva-gosvami/sat-sandarbha/§43.verification_note: IAST fragment not found in best-match source file (sanskrit/caitanya_gaudiya/jiva_sat_sandarbha.txt).

jiva-gosvami/sat-sandarbha/§44.sanskrit_iast: tad evaṃ māyāśrayatva-māyā-mohitatvābhyāṃ sthite dvayor bhede tad-bhajanasya eva abhidheyatvam āyātam.
jiva-gosvami/sat-sandarbha/§44.english_close: Thus, once the difference between the two stands established through the one’s being the locus of *māyā* and the other’s being deluded by *māyā*, it follows that the worship of him alone has the status of *abhidheya* (the operative means to be practiced).

jiva-gosvami/sat-sandarbha/§45.sanskrit_iast: ataḥ śrī-bhagavataḥ eva sarva-hitopadeṣṭṛtvāt, sarva-duḥkha-haratvāt, raśmīnāṃ sūryavat sarveṣāṃ parama-svarūpatvāt, sarvādhika-guṇa-śālitvāt, parama-prema-yogatvam iti prayojanaṃ ca sthāpitam.
jiva-gosvami/sat-sandarbha/§45.english_close: Therefore, it has also been established that Śrī Bhagavān alone is the proper object of *parama-prema* (supreme love). That too is the *prayojana* (consummating end): because he instructs the good of all; because he removes all suffering; because, as the sun is in relation to its rays, he is the highest intrinsic reality of all; and because he is endowed with qualities in the highest degree.
jiva-gosvami/sat-sandarbha/§45.verification_note: IAST fragment not found in best-match source file (sanskrit/caitanya_gaudiya/jiva_sat_sandarbha.txt).

kesava-kasmiri/krama-dipika/2.16.sanskrit_iast:
anayor gopī-janayoḥ samīraṇād āśrito vyāptyā |
vallabha ity upadiṣṭaṃ sāndrānandaṃ nirañjanaṃ jyotiḥ ||
kesava-kasmiri/krama-dipika/2.16.english_close: With reference to these two, *gopī* and *jana*, through impelling presence, supportive indwelling, and pervasion, the dense-bliss, stainless light is indicated by the designation “Vallabha.”

kesava-kasmiri/krama-dipika/2.17.sanskrit_iast:
svāhety ātmānaṃ gamayāmīty atejase tasmai |
yaḥ kārya-kāraṇeśaḥ paramātmety acyutaikatā asya manoḥ ||
kesava-kasmiri/krama-dipika/2.17.english_close: “‘Svāhā’ means: ‘I convey my very self to that radiance.’” “‘He who is the lord of cause and effect is the Supreme Self’: thus the purport of this mantra is unity with Acyuta.”

kesava-kasmiri/krama-dipika/6.4.sanskrit_iast:
manvor anyatareṇaivaṃ kuryād yaḥ susamāhitaḥ |
smṛtiṃ medhāṃ mati-balaṃ labdhvā sa kavirāḍ bhavet |
syān manus tat-sama-japa-dhyāna-homa-phalo 'paraḥ ||
kesava-kasmiri/krama-dipika/6.4.english_close: Whoever, well-collected, should thus proceed by means of either one of the two mantras, having obtained memory, intelligence, discernment, and strength, he would become a prince among poets. And another mantra should have the same fruit as that one in recitation, meditation, and oblation.

kesava-kasmiri/krama-dipika/6.5a.english_close: I always resort, as refuge, to the two feet of Śrī Mukunda.

kesava-kasmiri/krama-dipika/8.95.sanskrit_iast:
akhila-manuṣu mantrā vaiṣṇavā vīryavanto
mahitatara-phalāḍhyās teṣu gopāla-mantrāḥ |
prabalatara ihaiṣo 'mīṣu saṃmohanākhyo
manur anupama-sampat-kalpanākalpa-śākhī ||
kesava-kasmiri/krama-dipika/8.95.english_close: Among all mantras, the Vaiṣṇava mantras are possessed of power. Among them, the Gopāla-mantras are richer in especially exalted fruit. Among these, this so-called Saṃmohana-mantra is stronger still. This mantra is a wish-tree branch for bringing forth incomparable prosperity.

madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/1.sanskrit_iast:
māyākalpitamātṛtāmukhamṛṣādvaitaprapañcāśrayaḥ
satyajñānasukhātmakaḥ śrutiśikhotthākhaṇḍadhīgocaraḥ ।
mithyābandhavidhūnanena paramānandaikatānātmakaṃ
mokṣaṃ prāpta iva svayaṃ vijayate viṣṇurvikalpojjhitaḥ ॥ 1 ॥
madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/1.english_close: He whose substrate is the false dualistic manifold, beginning with knowerhood, imagined by *māyā* (cognitive projection), whose nature is reality, cognition, and bliss, and who is the object of the undivided cognition arising from the crest of scripture, by the shaking off of false bondage, as if having attained liberation consisting in an unbroken continuity of supreme bliss, Viṣṇu shines forth of himself, free from conceptual construction.

madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/2.sanskrit_iast:
śrīrāmaviśveśvaramādhavānāmaikyena sākṣātkṛtamādhavānām ।
sparśena nirdhūtatamorajobhyaḥ pādotthitebhyo 'stu namo rajobhyaḥ ॥ 2 ॥
madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/2.english_close: May there be reverence to the dust risen from the feet of those Mādhavas who have directly realized Mādhava through the unity of Śrī Rāma and Viśveśvara, and by whose touch darkness and the dust of passion are shaken away.

madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/3.sanskrit_iast:
bahubhirvihitā budhaiḥ parārthaṃ vijayante 'mitavistṛtā nibandhāḥ ।
mama tu śrama eṣa nūnamātmambharitāṃ bhāvayituṃ bhaviṣyatīha ॥ 3 ॥
madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/3.english_close: Vast treatises, composed by many learned men for the sake of others, flourish. But this labor of mine here will perhaps make me appear self-regarding.

madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/4.sanskrit_iast:
śraddhādhanena muninā madhusūdanena saṅgṛhya śāstranicayaṃ racitātiyatnāt ।
bodhāya vādivijayāya ca satvarāṇāmadvaitasiddhiriyamastu mude budhānām ॥ 4 ॥
madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/4.english_close: Having gathered the mass of śāstra, and composed it with extreme effort, by the sage Madhusūdana, rich in faith, may this *Advaita-Siddhi* be for the understanding and for the victory in debate of those who are eager, and for the delight of the learned.

madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.sanskrit_iast: tatrādvaitasiddher dvaitamithyātvasiddhipūrvakatvāt dvaitamithyātvam eva prathamam upapādanīyam ।
madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.english_close: Since the *Advaita-Siddhi* is preceded by the establishment of the falsity of duality, the falsity of duality itself must first be demonstrated.

madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.sanskrit_iast: upapādanaṃ ca svapakṣasādhanaparapakṣanirākaraṇābhyāṃ bhavatīti tadubhayaṃ vādajalpavitaṇḍānām anyatamāṃ kathām āśritya sampādanīyam ।
madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.english_close: And demonstration proceeds by the twofold procedure of establishing one’s own thesis and refuting the opponent’s thesis. Therefore that twofold work must be carried out by relying on one or another of the forms of disputation, namely *vāda*, *jalpa*, and *vitaṇḍā*.

madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.sanskrit_iast: tatra ca vipratipattijanyasaṃśayasya vicārāṅgatvān madhyasthenādau vipratipattiḥ pradarśanīyā ।
madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.english_close: And here, because a doubt generated by contradictory views is an element of inquiry, the contradictory thesis must first be set out by a neutral arbiter.

madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.sanskrit_iast: yadyapi vipratipattijanyasaṃśayasya na pakṣatāsampādakatayopayogaḥ, siṣādhayiṣāvirahasahakṛtasādhakamānābhāvarūpāyās tasyāḥ saṃśayāghaṭitatvāt ।
madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.english_close: Although the doubt produced by contradictory views is of no use for producing *pakṣatā* (the status of being the inferential subject), because that *pakṣatā* is constituted by the absence of a proving cognition, assisted by the absence of a desire-to-prove, and is not built out of doubt.

madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.sanskrit_iast: anyathā śrutyātmaniścayavato 'numitsayā tadanumānaṃ na syāt, vādyādīnāṃ niścayavattvena saṃśayāsambhavād āhāryasaṃśayasyātiprasañjakatvāc ca ।
madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.english_close: Otherwise, for one who already has certainty about the self from scripture, that inference would not arise even if he wished to infer. And, since disputants and the rest possess determinate convictions, doubt would be impossible; while a merely fabricated doubt would become over-extended.

madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.sanskrit_iast: nāpi vipratipatteḥ svarūpata eva pakṣapratipakṣaparigrahaphalakatayopayogaḥ, “tvayedaṃ sādhanīyam”, “anenedaṃ dūṣaṇīyam” ityādimadhyasthavākyād eva tallābhena vipratipattivaiyarthyāt ।
madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.english_close: Nor again does *vipratipatti* have, by its very own nature, any use as that which yields the adoption of thesis and counter-thesis. For that result is already obtained from the neutral arbiter’s statement, “this is to be proved by you,” “this is to be refuted by this,” and the like; and thus *vipratipatti* would be redundant.
Even so, although the doubt produced by conflicting views is not a limb of inference, it is still a limb of inquiry, because it is something to be cleared away.

madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.sanskrit_iast: tādṛśasaṃśayaṃ prati vipratipatteḥ kvacin niścayādipratibandhād ajanakatve 'pi svarūpayogyatvāt ।
madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.english_close: For, with respect to such a doubt, even if a conflicting thesis does not in some cases generate it, because of impediments such as prior certainty, still it is fit by its very nature to do so.

madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.sanskrit_iast: vādyādīnāṃ ca niścayavattve niyamābhāvāt “niścitau hi vādaṃ kuruta” ity ābhimānikaniścayābhiprāyaṃ parapakṣam ālambyāpy ahaṃkāriṇo viparītaniścayavato jalpādau pravṛttidarśanāt ।
madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.english_close: And there is no rule that disputants and the rest must possess settled certainty in just one way. “Debate, after all, is conducted under certainty” is to be taken as referring to merely presumed certainty, since one observes that even the self-assertive man, holding the opposite conviction, engages in wrangling and the like by taking up the opponent’s position.

madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.sanskrit_iast: tasmāt samayabandhādivat svakartavyanirvāhāya madhyasthena vipratipattiḥ pradarśanīyaiva ।
madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.english_close: Therefore, like the stipulation of procedural rules and the like, the contradictory thesis must indeed be stated by the neutral party, for the carrying out of his own task.

madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.sanskrit_iast: tatra mithyātve vipratipattiḥ brahmapramātiriktābādhyatve sati sattvena pratītyarhaṃ cidbhinnaṃ pratipannopādhau traikālikaniṣedhapratiyogi na vā, pāramārthikatvākāreṇokta-niṣedhapratiyogi na veti ।
madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/Prathama-pari.english_close: There, with regard to *mithyātva* (falsity), the contradictory thesis is this: whether that which is different from consciousness, fit to appear as existent, and not sublated by any cognition other than the cognition of Brahman, is the counter-positive of a three-time negation in a cognized locus, or instead is the counter-positive of the said negation under the aspect of being ultimately real.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1ab.sanskrit_iast: nava-rasa-militaṃ vā kevalaṃ vā pumarthaṃ / paramam iha mukunde bhakti-yogaṃ vadanti
madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1ab.english_close: Whether mixed with the nine *rasas* (aesthetic savors), or else existing in purity by itself, they call *bhakti-yoga* in Mukunda here the supreme human end.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1cd.sanskrit_iast: nirupama-sukha-saṃvid-rūpam aspṛṣṭa-duḥkhaṃ / tam aham akhila-tuṣṭyai śāstra-dṛṣṭyā vyanajmi
madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1cd.english_close: Having the form of incomparable bliss-consciousness, untouched by sorrow, that I manifest, by the vision of scripture, for the satisfaction of all.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.1.sanskrit_iast: nanu tarhi nāmāntareṇa brahma-vidyaiva bhagavad-bhaktir ity uktam
madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.1.english_close: Well then, has it been said that *bhagavad-bhakti* is simply *brahma-vidyā* under another name?

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.2.sanskrit_iast: tathā hi — tam etaṃ vedānuvacanena brāhmaṇā vividiṣanti yajñena dānena tapasā'nāśakena ity ādi śrutyā sarva-sukṛta-sādhyatvena brahma-vidyā pratipāditā | sarvāpekṣādhikaraṇe ca tathaiva nirṇītā | ihāpi pūrvodāhṛta-vacanaiḥ sarva-sukṛta-sādhyatvena lakṣaṇena bhagavad-bhakter brahma-vidyā-rūpatāyāḥ pratipādanāt tasyāś ca niratiśaya-puruṣārthatvasya caturlakṣaṇa-mīmāṃsayā apratipatti-vipratipatti-nivāraṇena vyavasthāpitatvād vyartho'yaṃ vicārārambha iti cet
madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.2.english_close: For thus: by the scriptural statement beginning "The Brāhmaṇas seek to know this by recitation of the Veda, by sacrifice, by gift, by austerity that is not destructive," *brahma-vidyā* has been taught as something accomplished by all meritorious acts. And in the *Sarvāpekṣādhikaraṇa* it has been determined in just that way. Here too, since by the previously cited passages the state of *bhagavad-bhakti* as itself *brahma-vidyā* has been established through the mark of being attainable by all meritorious acts, and since its status as a non-surpassable human end has already been fixed, by the fourfold inquiry, through the removal of non-apprehension and contrary apprehension, then this beginning of a further investigation is pointless.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.3.english_close: No. Because *bhakti* and *brahma-vidyā* differ with respect to intrinsic character, means, fruit, and eligible subject.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.4.sanskrit_iast: dravī-bhāva-pūrvikā hi manaso bhagavad-ākāratā savikalpaka-vṛtti-rūpā bhaktiḥ
madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.4.english_close: For *bhakti* is the mind's taking on the form of Bhagavat, preceded by melting affect, and having the character of a determinate mental modification.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.5.english_close: *Brahma-vidyā*, by contrast, is a non-conceptual mental modification, not accompanied by melted affect, whose range is the non-dual self alone.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.6.sanskrit_iast: bhagavad-guṇa-garimā-granthana-rūpa-grantha-śravaṇaṃ bhakti-sādhanam | tat tvam asyādi vedānta-mahā-vākyaṃ brahma-vidyā-sādhanam
madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.6.english_close: Hearing a text whose form is the tying together of the greatness of Bhagavat's qualities is the means to *bhakti*. The great Vedāntic sentence beginning with "tat tvam asi" is the means to *brahma-vidyā*.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.7.english_close: The intensification of love directed toward Bhagavat is the fruit of *bhakti*. The cessation of ignorance, root of all misfortune, is the fruit of *brahma-vidyā*.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.8.sanskrit_iast: prāṇi-mātrasya bhaktāv adhikāraḥ | brahma-vidyāyāṃ tu sādhana-catuṣṭaya-sampannasya paramahaṃsa-parivrājakasya
madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.8.english_close: For *bhakti*, every living being has eligibility. But for *brahma-vidyā*, only the *paramahaṃsa-parivrājaka* (supreme wandering renunciant) endowed with the fourfold discipline.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.9.sanskrit_iast: yajña-dānādi-sarva-sukṛta-sādhyatvaṃ tu samānaṃ bhakti-brahma-vidyayoḥ svarga-vividiṣayor iva | yathā, svarga-kāmo darśa-pūrṇamāsābhyāṃ yajeta iti sthita eva svarga-sādhanatve "svargebhyaḥ kāmebhyo darśa-paurṇamāsau" iti vākyena phalāntaratvam api bodhyate
madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.9.english_close: Yet the fact of being accomplished by all meritorious acts, beginning with sacrifice and gift, is common to *bhakti* and *brahma-vidyā*, just as it is to heaven and the desire-to-know. For, although it is already settled that one should sacrifice with the new- and full-moon rites when desiring heaven, the Vedic sentence "The darśa and paurṇamāsa rites are for the desires that concern heaven" also makes known a difference of result.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.10.sanskrit_iast: tathā, tam etaṃ vedānuvacanena [bṛ.ā.u. 4.4.22] ity ādinā vividiṣā-sādhanatvam api saṃyoga-pṛthaktva-nyāyena bodhyate, tathā bhakti-brahma-vidyayor api bhaviṣyati | sāmagryaikyaṃ hi kāryaikye na tu kāraṇa-mātraikye, atiprasaṅgāt | evaṃ bhakti-brahma-vidyayor api svarga-vividiṣayor iva phala-sādhana-bhāvābhāvaś ca tulya-sādhana-sādhyatvaṃ ca bhaviṣyati
madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.10.english_close: So too, by the text beginning "tam etaṃ vedānuvacanena," the status of being a means to the desire-to-know is made known by the principle of conjunction-with-distinct-allocation; the same will hold here also for *bhakti* and *brahma-vidyā*. For identity of total causal equipment obtains only where there is identity of effect, not merely identity of some cause; otherwise there would be over-extension. Thus, in the case of *bhakti* and *brahma-vidyā* too, as in the case of heaven and the desire-to-know, there will be both difference in the status of result and means, and equality in being attained through similar preparatory means.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.11.sanskrit_iast: nanu, brahma-vidyātiriktatve bhakteḥ svargādivan niratiśaya-puruṣārthatvaṃ na syād iti cen na, svargāder niyata-deśa-kāla-śarīrendriyādi-bhogyatvena sarvatropabhoktum aśakyatvāt kṣayitva-pāratantrya-lakṣaṇa-duḥkha-dvayānubiddhatvena niratiśayatvābhāve'pi bhakti-sukha-dhārāyāḥ sarva-deśa-kāla-śarīrendriyādi-sādhāraṇyena brahma-vidyā-phalavad upabhoktuṃ śakyatvāt kṣayitva-pāratantrya-lakṣaṇa-duḥkha-dvayānuvedhābhāvena niratiśayatvopapatteḥ
madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.11.english_close: If one says: if *bhakti* is distinct from *brahma-vidyā*, then, like heaven and the like, it cannot be an unsurpassed human end. No. Although heaven and the like lack unsurpassability, because they are enjoyables restricted to a particular place, time, body, senses, and so on, are therefore not enjoyably available everywhere, and are pierced by the two sorrows called perishability and dependence, still the stream of bliss that belongs to *bhakti* can be enjoyed, like the fruit of *brahma-vidyā*, because it is common to every place, time, body, and sense-condition, and because it is not penetrated by those two sorrows of perishability and dependence. Therefore its unsurpassability is intelligible.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.12.sanskrit_iast: tyaktvā svadharmaṃ caraṇāmbujaṃ harer bhajann apakvo 'tha patet tato yadi / yatra kva vābhadram abhūd amuṣya kiṃ ko vārtha āpto 'bhajatāṃ svadharmataḥ
madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.12.english_close: If, having abandoned one's own duty, one worships Hari's lotus feet, and then, still immature, should fall away from that, what misfortune has really befallen him anywhere? And what gain has been obtained by those who did not worship, merely through their own duty?

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.13.sanskrit_iast: tasyaiva hetoḥ prayateta kovido na labhyate yad bhramatām upary adhaḥ / tal labhyate duḥkhavad anyataḥ sukhaṃ kālena sarvatra gabhīra-raṃhasā
madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.13.english_close: For that very reason, the wise should strive for it; for what is not obtained by wanderers above and below [through saṃsāra], that is to be sought. Happiness from elsewhere comes of itself in time, everywhere, just as suffering does, with its deep rush.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.14.sanskrit_iast: na vai jano jātu kathañcanāvrajen mukunda-sevī anyavad aṅga saṃsṛtim / smaran mukundāṅghry-upagūhanaṃ punar vihātum icchen na rasa-graho janaḥ
madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.14.english_close: Truly, a person who has served Mukunda would never, in any way at all, re-enter saṃsṛti like other people, dear one. Remembering the embrace of Mukunda's feet, a person seized by the savor would not wish to abandon it again.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.15.sanskrit_iast: sakṛn manaḥ kṛṣṇa-padāravindayor niveśitaṃ tad-guṇa-rāgi yair iha / na te yamaṃ pāśa-bhṛtaś ca tad-bhaṭān svapne 'pi paśyanti hi cīrṇa-niṣkṛtāḥ
madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.15.english_close: Those whose mind has here been placed, even once, upon Kṛṣṇa's lotus feet, and has become attached to his qualities, they, their expiation already accomplished, do not see Yama, nor his noose-bearing servants, not even in a dream.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.16.sanskrit_iast: ata evāpattāv api duḥkhāsaṃsparśitva-pratipādanāyāspṛṣṭa-duḥkham iti viśeṣaṇam upāttam | ata eva ca na pariṇati-virasena svargādinā sāmyam | etena laukika-rasa-vailakṣaṇyam api vyākhyātam | tasyāpi śāstrāvihitatvena pāpa-kṣayāhetutvenāpattau duḥkha-saṃsparśitatvāt | bhaktes tu dṛṣṭādṛṣṭa-phalatayā mahān viśeṣo vakṣyate
madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.16.english_close: For precisely this reason, even in conditions of adversity, the qualifier "untouched by sorrow" was adopted in order to state its non-contact with pain. For that same reason, it is not on a level with heaven and the like, whose savor ends in transformation. By this, its distinctness from worldly *rasa* too has been explained. For even that [worldly *rasa*], though scripturally not forbidden and though a cause of diminishing sin, still comes into contact with sorrow when adversity arises. But in the case of *bhakti*, a great difference, as possessing both seen and unseen fruits, will be explained.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.17.sanskrit_iast: nanv evaṃ sati bhakti-sukhād vairāgyāsambhavena mumukṣutvāsambhavāt tad-adhikārika-catur-lakṣaṇa-mīmāṃsārambho na syād iti cet, satyam | bhakti-sukhāsaktān prati tasyānārambhāt
madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.17.english_close: If, this being so, one says that because dispassion cannot arise from devotional bliss, and therefore the desire for liberation cannot arise, the inquiry into the four marks of the qualified aspirant ought not to begin at all, that much is granted. For it is not begun with reference to those attached to the bliss of *bhakti*.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.18.sanskrit_iast: bhajanīya-svarūpa-nirṇayārthaṃ bhaktānām api tad-vicārasyāvaśyakatvāc ca | bhakti-sukhād vairāgyaṃ na syād iti tv iṣṭam eva nāpāditam
madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.18.english_close: Yet that inquiry is still necessary even for devotees, for the sake of determining the nature of what is to be worshipped. And as for the claim that dispassion does not arise from devotional bliss, that is no unwelcome consequence for us at all.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.19.sanskrit_iast: ātmārāmāś ca munayo nirgranthā apy urukrame / kurvanty ahaitukīṃ bhaktim ittham-bhūta-guṇo hariḥ
madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.19.english_close: Even sages who delight in the self, though free from all bonds, render causeless devotion to Urukrama. Such indeed are Hari's qualities.

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.20.english_close: Because, by this and similar passages, devotion to Bhagavat is taught even in the case of the *jīvan-muktas* (liberated while living).

madhusudana/bhakti-rasayana/1.1 ṭīkā.21.english_close: [Thus ends the exposition of the first kārikā, which has the form of a compendium of all the matters that will be stated later.]

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/Upodghāta, m.sanskrit_iast: śrīśaṅkarācāryanavāvatāraṃ viśveśvaraṃ viśvaguruṃ praṇamya / vedāntaśāstraśravaṇālasānāṃ bodhāya kurve kam api prayatnam //
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/Upodghāta, m.english_close: Having bowed to Viśveśvara, the Lord of the universe, the teacher of the world, the new descent as Śrī Śaṅkarācārya, I undertake a certain effort for the awakening of those who are sluggish in hearing the Vedānta-śāstra.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/1.sanskrit_iast: iha khalu sākṣātparamparayā vā sarvān eva jīvān samuddidhīrṣur bhagavān śrīmacchaṅkaro 'nātmabhyo vivekenātmānaṃ nityaśuddhabuddhamuktasvabhāvaṃ saṃkṣepeṇa bodhayituṃ daśaślokīṃ praṇināya /
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/1.english_close: Here indeed the Blessed Śrī Śaṅkara, wishing to lift up all beings, whether directly or by succession, composed the *Daśa-Ślokī* in order briefly to teach the self, whose nature is eternal, pure, awakened, and free, by discriminating it from non-selves.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/2.sanskrit_iast: nanv idaṅkārāspadebhyo 'nātmabhyo vivekenāhaṅkārāspadam ātmānaṃ sarvo loko 'ham asmīti pratyeti duḥkhaṃ cānubhavati /
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/2.english_close: But does not everyone, by discriminating the self, the locus of the "I"-cognition, from the non-selves, the loci of the "this"-cognitions, already apprehend it as "I am," and also experience pain?

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/3.sanskrit_iast: tena jñātajñāpakatvān niṣprayojanakatvāc ca ātmatattvapratipādanaṃ vyartham iti cet,
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/3.english_close: If it be said, therefore, that the teaching of the reality of the self is futile, because it merely makes known what is already known, and because it serves no purpose:

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/4.sanskrit_iast: na, cidbhāsyatvena lakṣaṇena idaṅkārāspadānām api dehendriyamanasāṃ pratibhāsato 'haṅkārāspadatvena tadavivekāt
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/4.english_close: No. Because body, senses, and mind, though they are loci of the "this"-cognition by the mark of being illumined by consciousness, also appear as loci of the "I"-cognition, there is non-discrimination of the self from them.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/5.sanskrit_iast: tena viśuddhe 'py ātmani duḥkhitvādyabhimānāt śāstrīyeṇaiva brahmatmaikyajñānena samūlasya tasya nivṛtteḥ /
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/5.english_close: Hence there is, even with regard to the pure self, the conceit "I am afflicted," and the like; and that very error is removed root and branch only by the scriptural knowledge of the identity of Brahman and self.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/6.sanskrit_iast: tasmād ajñātajñāpakatvāt saprayojanakatvāc cātmatattvapratipādanaṃ na vyartham /
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/6.english_close: Therefore the teaching of the reality of the self is not futile, because it makes known what is not yet known, and because it has a genuine purpose.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/7.sanskrit_iast: tasya cātmatattvasya "tattvam asi" "ahaṃ brahmāsmi" ityādivedāntamahāvākyam eva pramāpakam /
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/7.english_close: And for that reality of the self, the great Vedāntic sentences such as "tat tvam asi" and "ahaṃ brahmāsmi" alone are the means of valid cognition.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/8.sanskrit_iast: vākyaṃ ca padārthajñānadvāreṇaiva jñāpakam iti tattvampadārthayoḥ prakṛtavākyānukūlayor anyato 'siddhatvāt tāv api śāstreṇaiva pramātavyau yūpāhavanīyapadārthavat /
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/8.english_close: And a sentence reveals only by way of knowledge of its word-meanings. Therefore, since the meanings of the words *tat* and *tvam*, as suited to the sentence in question, are not established from elsewhere, those two too must be known from scripture alone, just as the meanings of *yūpa* and *āhavanīya* must be.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/9.sanskrit_iast: tataś ca "yato vā imāni bhūtāni jāyante yena vā jātāni jīvanti" ityādyāḥ sṛṣṭyādiśrutayas tatpadavācyārthasya samarpikāḥ "satyaṃ jñānam anantam" ityādyās tu lakṣyārthasya /
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/9.english_close: And therefore the śruti-passages beginning "From which indeed these beings are born, by which, once born, they live" present the direct denotation of the word *tat*; but those beginning "Reality, knowledge, infinity" present its indicated meaning.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/10.sanskrit_iast: evaṃ jāgratsvapnasuṣuptyādiśrutayaḥ "tad yathā mahāmatsya ubhe kūle anusañcarati" ityādyās tvampadavācyārthasya samarpikāḥ "yo 'yaṃ vijñānamayaḥ prāṇeṣu hṛdyantarjyotiḥ puruṣaḥ" "na dṛṣṭer draṣṭāraṃ paśyeḥ" ityādyās tu lakṣyārthasya /
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/10.english_close: In the same way, the śruti-passages concerning waking, dream, and deep sleep, such as "as a great fish moves along both banks," present the direct denotation of the word *tvam*; but passages such as "this person made of cognition, the inner light in the vital breaths, in the heart," and "one should not see the seer of seeing," present its indicated meaning.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/11.sanskrit_iast: tena prathamam avāntaravākyebhyo 'nubhūtayoḥ śuddhajīvabrahmaṇos tattvamasyādivākye mukhārthānvayānupapatter lakṣaṇayā smaraṇopapattiḥ /
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/11.english_close: Accordingly, first, from the subsidiary sentences there is the possibility of recollection, by implication, of the pure individual self and pure Brahman as previously experienced, because in the sentence "tat tvam asi" and the like a connection in the primary sense is impossible.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/12.sanskrit_iast: suṣuptau nirvikalpakasākṣicaitanyānubhavāṅgīkārāc ca advitīyabrahmajijñāpayiṣayā pravṛttānāṃ satyajñānādipadānām upādhiviśiṣṭacaitanye śaktatve 'pi caitanyamātre tātparyeṇa tatraiva tadaṃśe eva saṃskārodbodhāc ca /
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/12.english_close: And because one admits an experience of non-conceptual witness-consciousness in deep sleep; and because the words "reality," "knowledge," and the rest, though they may have their direct power with respect to consciousness qualified by limiting adjuncts, are employed with the intention of making the non-dual Brahman known, so that the latent impression is awakened precisely there, in consciousness alone, in that very aspect.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/13.sanskrit_iast: icchanti hi ākāśādipadād api nirvikalpakaṃ smaraṇaṃ tātparyādhīnatvāc chabdavṛtteḥ /
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/13.english_close: For they do indeed allow even from a word such as "ākāśa" a non-conceptual recollection, because the function of a word depends upon intended purport.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/14.english_close: By this, the appearance of knower and cognition within the understanding of the meaning of the *mahāvākya* is ruled out.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/15.english_close: And because non-cognitive samādhi is established by śruti and smṛti.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/16.sanskrit_iast: pārokṣyasadvitīyatvābhyāṃ ca na tattvampadārthamātrānubhavād eva kṛtakṛtyatā /
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/16.english_close: And, because of remoteness and duality, mere experience of the denoted meanings of *tat* and *tvam* does not by itself amount to the state of having done what had to be done.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/17.english_close: And, because the directly denoted meanings appear as different, there is no redundancy.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/18.english_close: And, because the indicated meaning is one, the sentence has an undivided meaning.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/19.sanskrit_iast: padajanyasya ca smaraṇasya nirvikalpalavākyārthānukūlasya nirvikalpakatvam anubhavavad evāviruddham /
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/19.english_close: And the non-conceptuality of the recollection produced by the words, being suited to the non-conceptual meaning of the sentence, is no contradiction, just as in experience itself.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/20.english_close: And in the case of conceptual sentence-understanding, the presentation of conceptual word-meanings is a necessary component.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/21.sanskrit_iast: prakṛte ca nirvikalpako vākyārthabodhas tasyaiva pramātvenājñānanivartanasāmarthyāt /
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/21.english_close: But in the present case the understanding of the sentence-meaning is non-conceptual, because precisely that has the capacity, as a valid cognition, to remove ignorance.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/22.sanskrit_iast: ato na lakṣyatāvacchedakam antareṇa lakṣaṇānupapattiḥ / prakṛtavākyārthānukūlapadārthopasthitir eva śaktilakṣaṇāsādhyatvāt /
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/22.english_close: Therefore there is no impossibility of implication even without positing a separate delimiter of indicatedness; for the presentation of word-meanings suited to the meaning of the sentence at hand is precisely what is to be accomplished by direct denotation and implication.

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/23.sanskrit_iast: nanu tarhi vedāntavākyebhya eva padārthopasthitau vākyārthabodhe ca sati tasya svata eva prāmāṇyāt tenājñānatatkāryanivṛttyanupapattau kiṃ vicāreṇeti cet,
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/23.english_close: But then, if from the Vedāntic sentences themselves there arises presentation of the word-meanings and understanding of the sentence-meaning, and if that cognition is valid in itself, what need is there for inquiry, especially when, by it, the removal of ignorance and its effects would seem already accounted for?

madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/24.sanskrit_iast: satyam, vedāntā yady api svataḥprāmāṇyān nirvikalpakam ātmasākṣātkāraṃ janayanti tathāpi mandamatīnāṃ vādivipratipattijasaṃśayapratibandhena tasyājñānanāśakatvāsāmarthyāt vicāreṇa tu saṃśayanivṛttau nirapavādam ajñānanivṛttir iti saṃśayabījabhūtavādivipratipattinirākaraṇārthaṃ vicāra ārabhyate /
madhusudana/siddhanta-bindu/24.english_close: True: the Vedānta-texts, by their intrinsic validity, do generate a non-conceptual realization of the self. Yet, for those of dull understanding, that realization is unable to destroy ignorance because it is obstructed by doubt arising from the conflicting views of disputants. But when doubt is removed by inquiry, the cessation of ignorance is unobstructed. Therefore inquiry is commenced for the sake of removing those conflicting views of disputants that serve as the seed of doubt.

madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/1.1..sanskrit_iast:
dravyaṃ karma ca kālaśca svabhāvo jīva eva ca
yadanugrahataḥ santi na santi yadupekṣayā //
madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/1.1..english_close: Substance, action, and time, intrinsic nature, and indeed the individual self as well, exist through whose favor; they do not exist through whose disregard.

madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/1.1..sanskrit_iast:
narte tvat kriyate kiñcidityāderna hariṃ vinā
jñānasvabhāvato 'pi syānmuktiḥ kasyāpi hi kvacit //
madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/1.1..english_close: From scriptural statements beginning "Nothing whatever is done without you," not apart from Hari, would there ever be liberation for anyone merely from the very nature of knowledge.

madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/1.1..sanskrit_iast:
ajñānāṃ jñānado viṣṇurjñānināṃ mokṣadaśca saḥ
ānandadaśca muktānāṃ sa evaiko janārdanaḥ //
madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/1.1..english_close: Viṣṇu is the giver of knowledge to the ignorant, and he is the giver of liberation to the knowers; and he is the giver of bliss to the liberated: that Janārdana alone.

madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/1.4..sanskrit_iast:
jīveśvarabhidā caiva jaḍeśvarabhidā tathā
jīvabhedo mithaścaiva jaḍajīvabhidā tathā
mithaśca jaḍabhedo yaḥ prapañco bhedapañcakaḥ //
madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/1.4..english_close: The difference between the *jīva* and Īśvara, and likewise the difference between the insentient and Īśvara; the mutual difference among *jīvas*, and likewise the difference between the insentient and the *jīva*; and the mutual difference among insentient things: this world-manifold is the fivefold complex of differences.

madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/1.4..sanskrit_iast:
so 'yaṃ satyo hyanādiśca sādiścennāśamāpnuyāt
dvaitaṃ na vidyata iti tasmādajñānināṃ matam //
madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/1.4..english_close: This, indeed, is real and beginningless; if it had a beginning, it would attain destruction. Therefore the doctrine "duality does not exist" is the view of the ignorant.

madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/2.3..sanskrit_iast:
śuddhaḥ sākṣī yadā siddho duḥkhitvaṃ vāryate katham
upajīvyapramāṇaṃ tad bhedagrāhakameva hi
ato jīveśayorbhedaḥ śrutisāmarthyasusthiraḥ //
madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/2.3..english_close: When the pure *sākṣī* (witness-consciousness) is established, how is suffering-status to be excluded? That foundational *pramāṇa* (means of knowledge) is indeed an apprehender of difference. Therefore the difference between the *jīva* and the Lord is made very firm by the force of scripture.

madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/2.3..sanskrit_iast:
tathāpi tu cidānandapūrvāstatsadṛśā guṇāḥ
sārasvarūpamasyāpi muktāvapyavaśiṣyate //
madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/2.3..english_close: Even so, qualities beginning with consciousness and bliss, similar to his, remain, even in liberation, as this one's essential form.

madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/2.3..sanskrit_iast:
ato 'bhedavadevaitāḥ śrutayaḥ pravadanti hi
paurāṇāni ca vākyāni sādṛśyābhedasaṃśrayāt //
madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/2.3..english_close: Therefore these scriptural statements indeed speak as though of non-difference, and the Purāṇic statements do so as well, because they rest on a non-difference grounded in similarity.

madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/2.3..sanskrit_iast:
sādṛśyācca pradhānatvāt svātantryādapicābhidām
āhurīśena jīvasya na svarūpābhidāṃ kvacit //
madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/2.3..english_close: And from similarity, from predominance, and also from sovereignty-independence, they posit a designation, of the *jīva* by the term "Īśa," never at any point a designation of identity in essence.

madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/3.3..sanskrit_iast:
vairāgyato bhaktidārḍhyaṃ tenopāsā yadā bhavet
āparokṣyaṃ bhaved viṣṇoriti pādakramo bhavet //
madhva/anuvyakhyana-bhasya-passages/3.3..english_close: From dispassion comes firmness of devotion; then, when worshipful contemplation arises through that, immediate realization of Viṣṇu arises: such is the sequence of this pāda.

madhva/anuvyakhyana/1.1.13.sanskrit_iast:
dravyaṃ karma ca kālaśca svabhāvo jīva eva ca
yadanugrahataḥ santi na santi yadupekṣayā //
madhva/anuvyakhyana/1.1.13.english_close: Substance, action, and time, intrinsic nature, and indeed the individual self as well, exist through whose favor; they do not exist through whose disregard.

madhva/anuvyakhyana/1.1.14.sanskrit_iast:
narte tvat kriyate kiñcidityāderna hariṃ vinā
jñānasvabhāvato 'pi syānmuktiḥ kasyāpi hi kvacit //
madhva/anuvyakhyana/1.1.14.english_close: From scriptural statements beginning "Nothing whatever is done without you," not apart from Hari, would there ever be liberation for anyone merely from the very nature of knowledge.

madhva/anuvyakhyana/1.1.15.sanskrit_iast:
ajñānāṃ jñānado viṣṇurjñānināṃ mokṣadaśca saḥ
ānandadaśca muktānāṃ sa evaiko janārdanaḥ //
madhva/anuvyakhyana/1.1.15.english_close: Viṣṇu is the giver of knowledge to the ignorant, and he is the giver of liberation to the knowers; and he is the giver of bliss to the liberated: that Janārdana alone.

madhva/anuvyakhyana/1.4.111.sanskrit_iast:
jīveśvarabhidā caiva jaḍeśvarabhidā tathā
jīvabhedo mithaścaiva jaḍajīvabhidā tathā
mithaśca jaḍabhedo yaḥ prapañco bhedapañcakaḥ //
madhva/anuvyakhyana/1.4.111.english_close: The difference between the *jīva* and Īśvara, and likewise the difference between the insentient and Īśvara; the mutual difference among *jīvas*, and likewise the difference between the insentient and the *jīva*; and the mutual difference among insentient things: this world-manifold is the fivefold complex of differences.

madhva/anuvyakhyana/1.4.112.sanskrit_iast:
so 'yaṃ satyo hyanādiśca sādiścennāśamāpnuyāt
dvaitaṃ na vidyata iti tasmādajñānināṃ matam //
madhva/anuvyakhyana/1.4.112.english_close: This, indeed, is real and beginningless; if it had a beginning, it would attain destruction. Therefore the doctrine "duality does not exist" is the view of the ignorant.

madhva/anuvyakhyana/2.3.66.sanskrit_iast:
śuddhaḥ sākṣī yadā siddho duḥkhitvaṃ vāryate katham
upajīvyapramāṇaṃ tad bhedagrāhakameva hi
ato jīveśayorbhedaḥ śrutisāmarthyasusthiraḥ //
madhva/anuvyakhyana/2.3.66.english_close: When the pure *sākṣī* (witness-consciousness) is established, how is suffering-status to be excluded? That foundational *pramāṇa* (means of knowledge) is indeed an apprehender of difference. Therefore the difference between the *jīva* and the Lord is made very firm by the force of scripture.

madhva/anuvyakhyana/2.3.67.sanskrit_iast:
tathāpi tu cidānandapūrvāstatsadṛśā guṇāḥ
sārasvarūpamasyāpi muktāvapyavaśiṣyate //
madhva/anuvyakhyana/2.3.67.english_close: Even so, qualities beginning with consciousness and bliss, similar to his, remain, even in liberation, as this one's essential form.

madhva/anuvyakhyana/2.3.68.sanskrit_iast:
ato 'bhedavadevaitāḥ śrutayaḥ pravadanti hi
paurāṇāni ca vākyāni sādṛśyābhedasaṃśrayāt //
madhva/anuvyakhyana/2.3.68.english_close: Therefore these scriptural statements indeed speak as though of non-difference, and the Purāṇic statements do so as well, because they rest on a non-difference grounded in similarity.

madhva/anuvyakhyana/2.3.69.sanskrit_iast:
sādṛśyācca pradhānatvāt svātantryādapicābhidām
āhurīśena jīvasya na svarūpābhidāṃ kvacit //
madhva/anuvyakhyana/2.3.69.english_close: And from similarity, from predominance, and also from sovereignty-independence, they posit a designation, of the *jīva* by the term "Īśa," never at any point a designation of identity in essence.

madhva/bhagavata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.1.2.sanskrit_iast: yaṃ pravrajantam anupetam apetakṛtyaṃ dvaipāyano virahakātara ājuhāva | putreti tan-mayatayā taravo 'bhineduḥ taṃ sarvabhūtahṛdayaṃ munim ānato 'smi ||
madhva/bhagavata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.1.2.english_close: I bow to that sage, the heart of all beings, who was going forth, unaccompanied, his obligations left behind. Dvaipāyana, made distraught by separation, called out, "My son!" So completely absorbed in him were they that even the trees answered.
"Unaccompanied" means: not accompanied by body and the rest.
Though not distressed, he made himself appear as though distressed.
He is "the heart of all beings" because he is of the nature of *ahaṅkāra*.

madhva/bhagavata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.1.3.sanskrit_iast: yaḥ svānubhāvam akhilaśrutisāram ekaṃ adhyātmadīpam atititīrṣatāṃ tamo 'ndham | saṃsāriṇāṃ karuṇayāha purāṇaguhyaṃ taṃ vyāsa-sūnum upayāmi guruṃ munīnām ||
madhva/bhagavata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.1.3.english_close: I approach that son of Vyāsa, the teacher of sages, who, out of compassion for transmigrating beings wishing to cross the blind darkness, spoke the secret of the Purāṇa, the one inner lamp, the essence of all revelation, the self-manifest power.

madhva/bhagavata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.1.4.sanskrit_iast: naimiṣe 'nimiṣakṣetre ṛṣayaḥ śaunakādayaḥ | satraṃ svargāya lokāya sahasrasamam āsata ||
madhva/bhagavata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.1.4.english_close: In Naimiṣa, the field of the Unblinking One, the seers beginning with Śaunaka sat in a session lasting a thousand years, for heaven, for the world.
The narrative episode is also for the removal of doubt regarding the human ends, by another mode.

madhva/bhagavata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.1.7.sanskrit_iast: yāni vedavidāṃ śreṣṭho bhagavān bādarāyaṇaḥ | anye ca munayaḥ sūta parāvaravido viduḥ ||
madhva/bhagavata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.1.7.english_close: Those things which the blessed Bādarāyaṇa, best among knowers of the Veda, knows, and which other sages too know, O Sūta, as knowers of the higher and the lower.

madhva/bhagavata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.1.8.sanskrit_iast: vettha tvaṃ saumya tat sarvaṃ tattvatas tadanugrahāt | brūyuḥ snigdhasya śiṣyasya guravo guhyam apy uta ||
madhva/bhagavata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.1.8.english_close: You know all that, gentle one, in its reality, by his favor. For teachers tell even what is secret to an affectionate disciple.

madhva/bhagavata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.1.7-.sanskrit_iast: yāni bhagavajjñātāni tāny anyair apy ṛṣibhir jñāyante. tāni vettha.
madhva/bhagavata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.1.7-.english_close: Those things that are known by Bhagavān are known also by other seers. Those are what you know.

madhva/bhagavata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.1.14.sanskrit_iast: āpannaḥ saṃsṛtiṃ ghorāṃ yannāma vivaśo gṛṇan | tataḥ sadyo vimucyeta yadbibheti svayaṃ bhayam ||
madhva/bhagavata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.1.14.english_close: One who has fallen into terrible transmigration, if helplessly uttering whose name, would at once be released from that; fear itself trembles before him.
"Helplessly" means: as the result of much repeated practice.

madhva/bhagavata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.1.18.sanskrit_iast: athākhyāhi harer dhīmann avatārakathāḥ śubhāḥ | līlā vidadhataḥ svairam īśvarasyātmamāyayā ||
madhva/bhagavata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.1.18.english_close: So now, wise one, tell the auspicious tales of Hari's descents, of the Lord, who performs his sportive acts freely by his own *ātma-māyā*.
"By his own *ātma-māyā*" means: by a will that belongs to his very nature.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.1.sanskrit_iast:
nārāyaṇaṃ guṇaiḥ sarvair udīrṇaṃ doṣavarjitam /
jñeyaṃ gamyaṃ gurūṃś cāpi natvā sūtrārtha ucyate //
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.1.english_close: Having bowed to Nārāyaṇa, exalted by all qualities and devoid of defects, to him who is to be known, to be attained, and also to the teachers, the meaning of the sūtras is stated.
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.1.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.2.sanskrit_iast: dvāpare sarvatra jñāne ākulībhūte tannirṇayāya brahmarudrendrādibhir arthito bhagavān nārāyaṇo vyāsatvenāvatatāra /
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.2.english_close: In the Dvāpara age, when knowledge had become confused everywhere, the Blessed Nārāyaṇa, requested by Brahmā, Rudra, Indra, and the others, descended in the form of Vyāsa for the determination of that.
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.2.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.3.sanskrit_iast: atheṣṭāniṣṭaprāptiparihārecchūnāṃ tadyogam avijānatāṃ tajjñāpanārthaṃ vedam utsannaṃ vyañjayaṃś caturdhā vyabhajat, caturviṃśatidhā ekaśatadhā sahasradhā dvādaśadhā ca /
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.3.english_close: Then, for those desiring the attainment of the desired and the avoidance of the undesired, who did not know the means to that, in order to make that known, while manifesting the Veda that had become obscured, he divided it into four, into twenty-four, into one hundred, into one thousand, and into twelve.
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.3.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.4.english_close: For the determination of that meaning, he composed the *Brahma-Sūtras*.
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.4.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.5.sanskrit_iast:
tac coktaṃ skānde -
nārāyaṇād viniṣpannaṃ jñānaṃ kṛtayuge sthitam /
kiñcit tad anyathā jātaṃ tretāyāṃ dvāpare 'khilam //
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.5.english_close: And that is said in the *Skanda* [Purāṇa]: knowledge, issued forth from Nārāyaṇa, stood in the Kṛta age; in the Tretā a part of it became altered; in the Dvāpara, all of it did.
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.5.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.6.sanskrit_iast:
gautamasya ṛṣeḥ śāpāj jñāne tv ajñānatāṃ gate /
saṅkīrṇabuddhayo devā brahmarudrapurassarāḥ /
śaraṇyaṃ śaraṇaṃ jagmuḥ nārāyaṇam anāmayam //
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.6.english_close: Because of the curse of the seer Gautama, when knowledge had gone over into non-knowledge, the gods, their understandings confused, with Brahmā and Rudra at their head, went for refuge to Nārāyaṇa, the one fit to be sought as refuge, the one free from affliction.
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.6.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.7.sanskrit_iast:
tair vijñāpitakāryas tu bhagavān puruṣottamaḥ /
avatīrṇo mahāyogī satyavatyāṃ parāśarāt /
utsannān bhagavān vedān ujjahāra hariḥ svayam /
caturdhā vyabhajat tāṃś ca caturviṃśatidhā punaḥ /
śatadhā caikadhā caiva tathaiva ca sahasradhā /
kṛṣṇo dvādaśadhā caiva
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.7.english_close: Then the Blessed Puruṣottama, whose task had been announced by them, descended, the great yogin, from Parāśara into Satyavatī; the Blessed Hari himself recovered the Vedas that had become obscured; and he divided them into four, and again into twenty-four; into one hundred, and indeed into one by one, and likewise into one thousand; Kṛṣṇa also into twelve.
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.7.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.8.english_close: again, for the understanding of its meaning.
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.8.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.9.sanskrit_iast:
alpākṣaram asandigdhaṃ sāravad viśvatomukham /
astobham anavadyaṃ ca sūtraṃ sūtravido viduḥ //
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.9.english_close: The knowers of sūtras know a sūtra as having few syllables, as free from doubt, as substantial, and as facing in every direction; as not padded, and as faultless.
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.9.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.10.sanskrit_iast:
nirviśeṣitasūtratvaṃ brahmasūtrasya cāpy ataḥ /
yathā vyāsatvam ekasya kṛṣṇasyānye viśeṣaṇāt //
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.10.english_close: And from this there is, indeed, the status of being the unqualified sūtra in the case of the *Brahma-Sūtra*; just as "Vyāsa-hood" belongs to one Kṛṣṇa, while for others there is specification.
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.10.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.11.english_close: For the learned know the other sūtras to be sūtras accompanied by qualifiers.
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.11.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.12.english_close: For the primary one, a word is used without qualification; for the others, with specification.
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.12.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.13.english_close: Thus speak the knowers of the Veda, those who know the reality and meaning of words.
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.13.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.14.english_close: In those sūtras in which all determinations have indeed been fully stated,
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.14.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.15.sanskrit_iast: śabdajātasya sarvasya yat pramāṇaś ca nirṇayaḥ /
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.15.english_close: in which there is the determination also of the authority of the whole body of verbal expression,
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.15.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.16.english_close: Having made sūtras of this kind, the greatly renowned Vyāsa,
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.16.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.17.english_close: among Brahmā, Rudra, and the other gods, among humans, ancestors, and birds,
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.17.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.18.sanskrit_iast: jñānaṃ saṃsthāpya bhagavān krīḍate puruṣottamaḥ / ityādi /
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.18.english_close: having established knowledge, the Blessed Puruṣottama sports. Thus and so forth.
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.18.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.19.english_close: Now, therefore: the inquiry into Brahman.
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.19.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.20.english_close: The word *atha* has the sense of auspicious beginning and also the sense of succession after qualification; the word *ataḥ* has the sense of cause or reason.
madhva/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1.20.verification_note: Primary text not yet on disk in clean form; locus is preserved, passage awaits acquisition.

madhva/karma-nirnaya/p. 257a.english_close: Action is to be performed, and it is to be preceded by devotion to the Lord, knowledge, and dispassion.

madhva/karma-nirnaya/p. 257b.sanskrit_iast:
naiṣkarmyam apy acyuta-bhāva-varjitaṃ na śobhate jñānam alaṃ nirañjanam
kutaḥ punaḥ śaśvad abhadram īśvare na cārpitaṃ karma yad apy akāraṇam
(*Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* 1.5.12, cited in the OCR witness)
madhva/karma-nirnaya/p. 257b.english_close: Even actionlessness, if devoid of orientation toward Acyuta, does not shine. Knowledge, though complete and stainless, does not shine either. How much less, then, action which is not offered to the Lord, action that remains perpetually inauspicious, even if it be otherwise without ulterior motive.

madhva/karma-nirnaya/p. 257c.sanskrit_iast:
yataḥ pravṛttir bhūtānāṃ yena sarvam idaṃ tatam
sva-karmaṇā tam abhyarcya siddhiṃ vindati mānavaḥ
(*Bhagavad-Gītā* 18.46, cited in the OCR witness)
madhva/karma-nirnaya/p. 257c.english_close: From whom there is the activity of beings, by whom this whole world is pervaded, by worshipping him through one's own action, a human being attains perfection.

madhva/karma-nirnaya/p. 257d.sanskrit_iast:
mayi sarvāṇi karmāṇi sannyasyādhyātma-cetasā
nirāśīr nirmamo bhūtvā yudhyasva vigata-jvaraḥ
(*Bhagavad-Gītā* 3.30, cited in the OCR witness)
madhva/karma-nirnaya/p. 257d.english_close: Renouncing all actions into me, with a mind turned to the self, having become free from expectation and free from possessiveness, fight, with fever gone.

madhva/karma-nirnaya/p. 257e.sanskrit_iast:
ye me matam idaṃ nityam anutiṣṭhanti mānavāḥ
śraddhāvanto 'nasūyanto mucyante te 'pi karmabhiḥ
(*Bhagavad-Gītā* 3.31, cited in the OCR witness)
madhva/karma-nirnaya/p. 257e.english_close: Those human beings who constantly follow this teaching of mine, being possessed of faith and not finding fault, they too are released from actions.

madhva/karma-nirnaya/p. 257f.sanskrit_iast:
ye tv etad abhyasūyanto nānutiṣṭhanti me matam
sarva-jñāna-vimūḍhāṃs tān viddhi naṣṭān acetasaḥ
(*Bhagavad-Gītā* 3.32, cited in the OCR witness)
madhva/karma-nirnaya/p. 257f.english_close: But those who, fault-finding with this, do not follow my teaching, know them to be deluded with respect to all knowledge, ruined, and without understanding.

madhva/karma-nirnaya/p. 258 / colophon-c.sanskrit_iast:
man-manā bhava mad-bhakto mad-yājī māṃ namaskuru
mām evaiṣyasi satyaṃ te pratijāne priyo 'si me
(*Bhagavad-Gītā* 18.65, cited in the OCR witness immediately before the colophon)
madhva/karma-nirnaya/p. 258 / colophon-c.english_close: Be one whose mind is on me. Be my devotee. Be one who sacrifices to me. Bow to me. You shall come to me alone. This is true. I promise it to you. You are dear to me.

madhva/mahabharata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.1.sanskrit_iast: nārāyaṇāya paripūrṇaguṇārṇavāya viśvodayasthitilayonniyatipradāya | jñānapradāya vibudhāsurasaukhyaduḥkhasatkāraṇāya vitatāya namo namaste ||
madhva/mahabharata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.1.english_close: Reverence to Nārāyaṇa, to the ocean of completely perfect qualities, to the giver of the universe's arising, maintenance, dissolution, and regulation. To the giver of knowledge, to the true cause of the happiness and suffering of gods and anti-gods, to the all-pervading one: obeisance, repeated obeisance.

madhva/mahabharata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.2.sanskrit_iast: āsīd udāraguṇavāridhir aprameyo nārāyaṇaḥ paratamaḥ paramāt sa ekaḥ | saṃśāntasaṃvid akhilaṃ jaṭhare nidhāya lakṣmībhujāntaragataḥ svarataḥ api cāgre ||
madhva/mahabharata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.2.english_close: There was Nārāyaṇa alone, immeasurable, an ocean of noble qualities, the highest one beyond the high. Having placed the whole [world], its consciousness brought to quiescence, within his belly, and having entered within Lakṣmī's embrace, he existed before [creation] too, delighting in himself.
madhva/mahabharata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.2.verification_note: IAST fragment not found in best-match source file (sanskrit/vedanta/madhva_mahabharata_tatparya.txt).

madhva/mahabharata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.3.sanskrit_iast: tasyodarasthajagataḥ sadamandasāndrasvānandatuṣṭavapuṣo 'pi ramāramasya | bhūtyai nijāśritajanasya hi sṛjyasṛṣṭau īkṣā babhūva paranāmanimeṣakānte ||
madhva/mahabharata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.3.english_close: Although he, the lover of Ramā, whose body was content in his own dense and undiminished bliss, had the world abiding in his belly, still, for the welfare of the people who had taken refuge in him, there arose an intentional glance toward the creation-to-be, at the end of the contraction called *nimeṣa* in the supreme state.

madhva/mahabharata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.4.sanskrit_iast: dṛṣṭvā sa cetanagaṇān jaṭhare śayānān ānandamātravapuṣaḥ sṛtivipramuktān | dhyānaṃ gatān sṛtigatāṃś ca suṣuptisaṃsthān brahmādikān kaliparān manujāṃs tathaikṣat ||
madhva/mahabharata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.4.english_close: Having seen the groups of sentient beings lying in his belly, whose bodies were sheer bliss, freed entirely from transmigration, and gone into meditation, and having seen also those who had entered transmigration, abiding as if in deep sleep, from Brahmā downward to the men subject to Kali, he beheld them.

madhva/mahabharata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.5.sanskrit_iast: srakṣye hi cetanagaṇān sukhaduḥkhamadhyasamprāptaye tanubhṛtāṃ vihṛtiṃ mama icchan | so 'yaṃ vihāra iha me tanubhṛtsvabhāvasambhūtaye bhavati bhūtikṛd eva bhūtyāḥ ||
madhva/mahabharata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.5.english_close: "I shall indeed create the groups of sentient beings for the attaining of the middle field between pleasure and pain, desiring my sport among embodied beings." "This sport of mine here becomes, precisely for the bringing forth of the natures of embodied beings, the very maker of their good."

madhva/mahabharata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.6.sanskrit_iast: itthaṃ vicintya paramaḥ sa tu vāsudevanāmā babhūva nijamuktipadapradātā | tasyājñayā eva niyatā atha ramā api rūpaṃ babhre dvitīyam api yat pravadanti māyām ||
madhva/mahabharata-tatparya-nirnaya/1.6.english_close: Having thus reflected, that supreme one then became the form named Vāsudeva, the giver of his own state of liberation. And Ramā too, under his command alone and in fixed conformity to it, assumed a second form, which they call Māyā.

madhva/mayavada-khandana/benediction.english_close: Narasiṃha, the sun that dispels the darkness constituted by every doctrine of ignorance, triumphs; he is an unfathomable ocean of true knowledge, bliss, and power.

madhva/mayavada-khandana/thesis.sanskrit_iast: vimatam anārambhaṇīyam, anyathā-pratipādakatvāt, yad itthaṃ tat tathā, yathā sampratipannam.
madhva/mayavada-khandana/thesis.english_close: The disputed thesis is not fit to be undertaken, because it presents matters otherwise than they are. Whatever is of such a kind is indeed so, as is presently admitted.

madhva/mayavada-khandana/genuine identit.locus: No genuine identity-thesis on the opponent's side (p. 238)
madhva/mayavada-khandana/genuine identit.english_close: For on that side there is no reality to the identity of Brahman and self.
madhva/mayavada-khandana/genuine identit.source: full_translations/madhva__mayavada-khandana.md#No genuine identity-thesis on the opponent's side (p. 238)

madhva/mayavada-khandana/the loss of non.locus: If the loss of non-duality stands apart from the self's nature (p. 238)
madhva/mayavada-khandana/the loss of non.english_close: If the loss of non-duality is something over and above the self's own nature,
madhva/mayavada-khandana/the loss of non.source: full_translations/madhva__mayavada-khandana.md#If the loss of non-duality stands apart from the self's nature (p. 238)

madhva/mayavada-khandana/it does not sta.locus: If it does not stand apart (p. 238)
madhva/mayavada-khandana/it does not sta.english_close: If it does not stand apart, then, because the self is self-luminous, there is the fault of proving what is already established.

madhva/mayavada-khandana/previously unas.english_close: And because the self is without differentiating features, no as-yet-unascertained differentiator remains in it.

madhva/mayavada-khandana/can serve as a.locus: Nothing can serve as a covering ignorance (p. 238)
madhva/mayavada-khandana/can serve as a.sanskrit_iast: siddhatvāt svarūpasya viśeṣābhāvāc ca nājñānaṃ kasyacid āvarakam.
madhva/mayavada-khandana/can serve as a.english_close: Because the intrinsic nature is already established, and because there is an absence of distinguishing feature, no ignorance can be a covering of anything whatsoever.
madhva/mayavada-khandana/can serve as a.source: full_translations/madhva__mayavada-khandana.md#Nothing can serve as a covering ignorance (p. 238)

madhva/mayavada-khandana/opponent's own.locus: The opponent's own definition of pramāṇa (p. 238)
madhva/mayavada-khandana/opponent's own.english_close: And their own doctrine says: "A *pramāṇa* is that which reaches an as-yet-unapprehended object."

madhva/mayavada-khandana/the whole doctr.locus: Therefore the whole doctrine is set aside (p. 238)
madhva/mayavada-khandana/the whole doctr.english_close: Precisely because ignorance is impossible, that entire doctrine has been set aside.
madhva/mayavada-khandana/the whole doctr.source: full_translations/madhva__mayavada-khandana.md#Therefore the whole doctrine is set aside (p. 238)

madhva/mayavada-khandana/would fail to d.locus: Scripture would fail to disclose reality (p. 238)
madhva/mayavada-khandana/would fail to d.english_close: And if identity were *mithyā*, scripture would have the status of not making reality known.

madhva/mayavada-khandana/would instead b.english_close: And the truth-status would belong to difference.

madhva/mayavada-khandana/stated purpose.english_close: In just this way, the purpose too is undone.

madhva/mayavada-khandana/would already b.english_close: Because liberation is of the nature of one's own intrinsic being, it would already be established beforehand.

madhva/mayavada-khandana/\"fifth kind\".locus: The "fifth kind" is excluded with ignorance itself (p. 238)
madhva/mayavada-khandana/\"fifth kind\".english_close: By the impossibility of ignorance, and by the absence of a fourth category, even the status of being a fifth category is ruled out.
madhva/mayavada-khandana/\"fifth kind\".source: full_translations/madhva__mayavada-khandana.md#The "fifth kind" is excluded with ignorance itself (p. 238)

madhva/mayavada-khandana/object and purp.locus: Without object and purpose, there is no qualified subject (p. 238)
madhva/mayavada-khandana/object and purp.english_close: And, precisely because object and purpose are absent, the qualified subject also is absent.
madhva/mayavada-khandana/object and purp.source: full_translations/madhva__mayavada-khandana.md#Without object and purpose, there is no qualified subject (p. 238)

madhva/mayavada-khandana/with that, no r.locus: And with that, no relation either (p. 238)
madhva/mayavada-khandana/with that, no r.english_close: And because that is absent, relation too is absent.

madhva/mayavada-khandana/15.16.sanskrit_iast:
dvāv imau puruṣau loke kṣaraś cākṣara eva ca
kṣaraḥ sarvāṇi bhūtāni kūṭastho 'kṣara ucyate //
madhva/mayavada-khandana/15.16.english_close: In the world there are these two persons: the perishable and indeed the imperishable. The perishable is all beings; the steadfast one is called the imperishable.

madhva/mayavada-khandana/15.17.sanskrit_iast:
uttamaḥ puruṣas tv anyaḥ paramātmety udāhṛtaḥ
yo loka-trayam āviśya bibharty avyaya īśvaraḥ //
madhva/mayavada-khandana/15.17.english_close: But another person is the highest, spoken of as the supreme self, the imperishable Lord who, entering the three worlds, sustains them.

madhva/mayavada-khandana/15.18.sanskrit_iast:
yasmāt kṣaram atīto 'ham akṣarād api cottamaḥ
ato 'smi loke vede ca prathitaḥ puruṣottamaḥ //
madhva/mayavada-khandana/15.18.english_close: Because I transcend the perishable, and am even higher than the imperishable, therefore I am proclaimed in the world and in the Veda as the supreme person.

madhva/mayavada-khandana/15.19.sanskrit_iast:
yo mām evam asammūḍho jānāti puruṣottamam
sa sarva-vid bhajati māṃ sarva-bhāvena bhārata //
madhva/mayavada-khandana/15.19.english_close: Whoever, undeluded, thus knows me as the supreme person, he, being all-knowing in this respect, worships me with his whole being, O Bhārata.

madhva/mayavada-khandana/15.20.sanskrit_iast:
iti guhyatamaṃ śāstram idam uktaṃ mayānagha
etad buddhvā buddhimān syāt kṛta-kṛtyaś ca bhārata //
madhva/mayavada-khandana/15.20.english_close: Thus has this most secret teaching been spoken by me, O sinless one; having understood this, one would become wise and one whose task is done, O Bhārata.

madhva/mayavada-khandana/Upaniṣad 1.3.10.sanskrit_iast:
indriyebhyaḥ parā hy arthā arthebhyaś ca paraṃ manaḥ
manasas tu parā buddhir buddher ātmā mahān paraḥ //
madhva/mayavada-khandana/Upaniṣad 1.3.10.english_close: Higher than the senses, indeed, are the objects; and higher than the objects is the mind. Higher than the mind is the intellect; higher than the intellect is the great self-principle.

madhva/mayavada-khandana/Upaniṣad 1.3.11.sanskrit_iast:
mahataḥ param avyaktam avyaktāt puruṣaḥ paraḥ
puruṣān na paraṃ kiñcit sā kāṣṭhā sā parā gatiḥ //
madhva/mayavada-khandana/Upaniṣad 1.3.11.english_close: Higher than the great principle is the unmanifest; higher than the unmanifest is the person. Beyond the person there is nothing further; that is the limit, that is the highest goal.

madhva/mayavada-khandana/purport: Viṣṇu'.sanskrit_iast: "bhūmnaḥ kratuvaj jyāyas tvaṃ tathā ca darśayati" iti viṣṇoḥ sarvottamatvam eva sarva-śāstrārthatvena bhagavatā śrutyā cābhihitam.
madhva/mayavada-khandana/purport: Viṣṇu'.english_close: "'You are greater, like the sacrificial act, than the Fullness' - thus too it shows." In this way, Viṣṇu's very status as the highest of all is declared both by the Blessed Lord and by scripture as the meaning of all the śāstras.

madhva/mayavada-khandana/verse of refuta.sanskrit_iast:
iti sarvajña-muninā māyāvāda-tamo 'khilam
nirastaṃ tattva-vādena satāṃ saṃśaya-nuttaye //
madhva/mayavada-khandana/verse of refuta.english_close: Thus, by the omniscient sage, the whole darkness that is Māyāvāda has been dispelled by the doctrine of reality, for the removal of the good people's doubt.

madhva/mayavada-khandana/vow.sanskrit_iast:
nāsti nārāyaṇa-samaṃ na bhūtaṃ na bhaviṣyati
etena satya-vākyena sarvārthān sādhayāmy aham //
madhva/mayavada-khandana/vow.english_close: There is nothing equal to Nārāyaṇa; there has not been, and there will not be. By this true utterance I establish all the matters at issue.

madhva/mithyatvanumana-khandana/239, cla.sanskrit_iast: vimataṃ mithyā dṛśyatvād ity atra tat tathā yathā saṃpratipannam
madhva/mithyatvanumana-khandana/239, cla.english_close: In the inference "the disputed thing is *mithyā* because it is perceived," the subject is [to be taken as] that thing exactly as it is apprehended in that way.
If it is stated thus, then because the world would be absent, the subject is one whose substrate is unestablished.
Because the indeterminable has not been established, it has an unestablished qualifier.
If *mithyātva* is taken as being different from the real and the unreal, there is the fault of proving what is already granted.
Because that is absent, the reason is unestablished.
Precisely because the indeterminable is not established, and because there is no positive similar instance, the reason is contradictory.
Since even what is unreal may be perceived, the reason is inconclusive.
If the world is absent, then the inference too would be absent; thus, because it is overruled by reasoning, it is not determinately established.

madhva/mithyatvanumana-khandana/239, cla.sanskrit_iast: pratyakṣādiviruddhatvād idaṃ satyam ityādivākyaviruddhatvāc ca kālātyayāpadiṣṭaḥ
madhva/mithyatvanumana-khandana/239, cla.english_close: Because it conflicts with perception and the other means of knowledge, and because it conflicts with scriptural statements such as "this is real," it is a reason stated after its proper time, one already defeated by later knowledge.

madhva/mithyatvanumana-khandana/239, cla.sanskrit_iast: rajataṃ sat iti bhramamātratvād vimataṃ sad dṛśyatvād asadvad ity api prayoge smaraṇasamaḥ
madhva/mithyatvanumana-khandana/239, cla.english_close: Since "the silver is real" is only an erroneous cognition, even the counter-formulation "the disputed thing is real because it is perceived, like the unreal" comes out merely on a par with a memory-case.

madhva/mithyatvanumana-khandana/239, cla.sanskrit_iast: vimataṃ sat pramāṇadṛṣṭatvād ity atra tat tathā yathā sad iti prayogāt satyasādhanaḥ
madhva/mithyatvanumana-khandana/239, cla.english_close: In the inference "the disputed thing is real because it is seen by a valid means of knowledge," if one formulates it as "that, exactly thus, is real," the inference proves reality.
Since even shell-silver lacks indeterminability, the reason is defective through absence of the probandum in the example.
And, in the manner stated, because perceptibility is absent, it is also defective in respect of the proving reason itself.
Conflict with a valid means of knowledge is an *upādhi* (a defeating condition).
The subject is that which is qualified by the property to be proved.
The positive similar instance is that which is qualified by a property similar to the probandum.
The negative counter-instance is that which is qualified by a property contrary to the probandum.
The statement of the subject is the proposition.
The sign is the reason.
The illustrative showing is the example.

madhva/mithyatvanumana-khandana/239, clo.sanskrit_iast:
yo hy avyakta-sadānanda-nityavyakta-cidātmanā
nirdoṣākhila-kalyāṇa-guṇaṃ vande ramāpatim //
madhva/mithyatvanumana-khandana/239, clo.english_close: Him indeed whose conscious self is unmanifest bliss, yet eternally manifest consciousness, him, flawless and possessed of every auspicious quality, I praise: the Lord of Ramā.

madhva/tattva-sankhyana/1.sanskrit_iast:
svatantram asvatantraṃ ca dvividhaṃ tattvam iṣyate |
svatantro bhagavān viṣṇuḥ, bhāvābhāvau dvidhā itarat || 1 ||
madhva/tattva-sankhyana/1.english_close: Reality is held to be twofold: the independent and the dependent. The independent is the Blessed Lord Viṣṇu. The other, the dependent, is twofold. It is being and non-being.

madhva/tattva-sankhyana/2.sanskrit_iast:
prākpradhvaṃsasadātvena trividho 'bhāva iṣyate |
cetanācetanatvena bhāvo 'pi dvividho mataḥ || 2 ||
madhva/tattva-sankhyana/2.english_close: Non-being is held to be threefold: as prior, as posterior-destruction, and as perpetual. Being too is regarded as twofold. That is by reason of sentientness and insentientness.

madhva/tattva-sankhyana/3.sanskrit_iast:
duḥkhaspṛṣṭaṃ tadaspṛṣṭam iti dvedhā eva cetanam |
nityāduḥkhā ramā, anye tu spṛṣṭaduḥkhāḥ samastaśaḥ || 3 ||
madhva/tattva-sankhyana/3.english_close: The sentient is just twofold: that touched by suffering and that not touched by it. Ramā is eternally without suffering. But all the others are altogether touched by suffering.

madhva/tattva-sankhyana/4.sanskrit_iast:
spṛṣṭaduḥkhā vimuktāś ca duḥkhasaṃsthā iti dvidhā |
duḥkhasaṃsthā muktiyogyā ayogyā iti ca dvidhā || 4 ||
madhva/tattva-sankhyana/4.english_close: Those touched by suffering are of two kinds: the liberated, and those established in suffering. Those established in suffering are of two kinds. They are those fit for liberation and those unfit.

madhva/tattva-sankhyana/5.sanskrit_iast:
devarṣi-pitṛ-pā narā iti muktās tu pañcadhā |
evaṃ vimuktiyogyāś ca tamoyogyāḥ sṛtisaṃsthitāḥ || 5 ||
madhva/tattva-sankhyana/5.english_close: The liberated are fivefold: gods, seers, fathers, protectors, and men. So too the release-fit [are fivefold]. The unfit are the darkness-fit and those established in the current [of transmigration].

madhva/tattva-sankhyana/6.sanskrit_iast:
daitya-rakṣaḥ-piśācakāḥ martyādhamāś caturdhā eva |
tamoyogyāḥ prakīrtitāḥ || 6 ||
madhva/tattva-sankhyana/6.english_close: The darkness-fit are proclaimed to be fourfold: Daityas, Rākṣasas, Piśācas, and the lowest among humans.

madhva/tattva-sankhyana/7.sanskrit_iast:
te ca prāptāndhatamasaḥ sṛtisaṃsthā iti dvidhā |
nityānityavibhāgena tridhā eva acetanaṃ matam || 7 ||
madhva/tattva-sankhyana/7.english_close: And those [darkness-fit beings] are of two kinds: those who have reached blind darkness, and those established in the current [toward it]. By division into eternal, eternal-non-eternal, and non-eternal, the insentient too is held to be threefold.

madhva/tattva-sankhyana/8.sanskrit_iast:
nityā vedāḥ purāṇādyāḥ kālaḥ prakṛtir eva ca |
nityānityaṃ tridhā proktam anityaṃ dvividhaṃ matam || 8 ||
madhva/tattva-sankhyana/8.english_close: The Vedas are eternal. The eternal-non-eternal is said to be threefold: the Purāṇas and the like, time, and primal matter. The non-eternal is held to be twofold.

madhva/tattva-sankhyana/9.sanskrit_iast:
asaṃsṛṣṭaṃ ca saṃsṛṣṭam | asaṃsṛṣṭaṃ mahān ahaṃ |
buddhir manaḥ khāni daśa mātrā bhūtāni pañca ca || 9 ||
madhva/tattva-sankhyana/9.english_close: The non-eternal is of two kinds: the not-wholly-produced and the wholly-produced. The not-wholly-produced are the Great Principle and ego-principle, intellect and mind, the ten sense-faculties, the subtle elements and the five gross elements.

madhva/tattva-sankhyana/10.sanskrit_iast:
saṃsṛṣṭam aṇḍaṃ tadvat ca samastaṃ saṃprakīrtitam |
sṛṣṭiḥ sthitiḥ saṃhṛtiś ca niyamo 'jñānabodhane || 10 ||
madhva/tattva-sankhyana/10.english_close: The wholly-produced is the cosmic egg, and likewise all that is within it. Creation, maintenance, and destruction; regulation, and ignorance and knowledge—

madhva/tattva-sankhyana/11.sanskrit_iast:
bandho mokṣaḥ sukhaṃ duḥkham āvṛtir jyotir eva ca |
viṣṇunāsya samastasya samāsavyāsayogataḥ || 11 ||
madhva/tattva-sankhyana/11.english_close: Bondage and liberation, pleasure and pain, concealment and manifestation as well— all this entire order is from Viṣṇu, both in compendious statement and in detailed distribution.

madhva/tattvodyota/Verse, line 1.english_close: Om. He whose power extends over all real things everywhere, independent, seeing all without remainder.

madhva/tattvodyota/Verse, line 2.english_close: May the Lord of Ramā, eternally of just such a sort, the terminus of conscious knowers and knowables, be our chosen deity.

madhva/tattvodyota/'numānam.english_close: Inference for difference in the liberated state.

madhva/tattvodyota/Statement.sanskrit_iast: vimato bhinnaḥ, muktatvāt, yad itthaṃ tat tathā, yathā sampratipannaḥ।
madhva/tattvodyota/Statement.english_close: The disputed subject is distinct, because it is liberated. Whatever is like this is so, just as in the admitted case.

madhva/tattvodyota/Sādhyavikalpanirākara.english_close: Therein: the elimination of alternative construals of the probandum.

madhva/tattvodyota/Third Option in Diffe.english_close: And no alternative option is tenable with respect to difference.

madhva/tattvodyota/Is Nowhere Establishe.english_close: Precisely because the “indefinable” is nowhere established.

madhva/tattvodyota/Hare's Horn, Cow's Ho.english_close: Just as one says “a hare's horn” and “a cow's horn.”
