abhinavagupta/ishvara-pratyabhijna-vimar.english_close: Appearance, though one object has been established by synthesis, differs according to taste, need, and learned usage. How then is there one svalakṣaṇa? It is said: when the separately appearing manifestations are animated by reflective synthesis, then that single thing is the svalakṣaṇa.

abhinavagupta/ishvara-pratyabhijna-vimar.sanskrit_iast: sā tu deśādikādhyakṣāntarabhinne svalakṣaṇe / tātkālikī pravṛttiḥ syād arthinaḥ // ... naikaikataḥ pramāṇāt sā pravṛttiḥ api tu pramāṇasamūhādeva
abhinavagupta/ishvara-pratyabhijna-vimar.english_close: That immediate activity of the desirer takes place with respect to the svalakṣaṇa not different from the other perceptions such as place and the rest. Such activity does not arise from any single means of knowledge, but from the aggregate of means of knowledge.

sankara/brahma-sutra-bhasya/2.1.14.sanskrit_iast: tadananyatvam ārambhaṇaśabdādibhyaḥ | ... kāryamākāśādikaṃ bahuprapañcaṃ jagat, kāraṇaṃ paraṃbrahma, tasmātkāraṇāt paramārthato 'nanyatvaṃ vyatirekeṇābhāvaḥ kāryasyāvagamyate ... 'vācārambhaṇaṃ vikāro nāmadheyaṃ mṛttiketyeva satyam' ... vācārambhaṇaśabdena ca vikārajātasyānṛtatvābhidhānāt.
sankara/brahma-sutra-bhasya/2.1.14.english_close: The effect-world is non-different from the supreme Brahman as cause; apart from the cause the effect has no independent reality. The modification is only a name beginning in speech, and the whole class of modifications is declared unreal.

ramanuja/vedartha-samgraha/§8.sanskrit_iast: apare tv apahatapāpmatvādisamastakalyāṇaguṇopetam api brahmaitenaivāikyāvabodhena kenacid upādhiviśeṣeṇa saṃbaddhaṃ badhyate mucyate ca nānāvidhamalarūpapariṇāmāspadaṃ ceti vyavasthitāḥ.
ramanuja/vedartha-samgraha/§8.english_close: Others hold that Brahman, though endowed with all auspicious qualities, is by this very cognition of identity connected with a certain limiting adjunct, becomes bound and released, and is the locus of manifold transformations of impure form.

ramanuja/vedartha-samgraha/§9.sanskrit_iast: anye punar aikyāvabodhayāthātmyaṃ varṇayantaḥ svābhāvikaniratiśayāparimitodāraguṇasāgaraṃ brahmaiva ... svabhāvato vilakṣaṇam avilakṣaṇaṃ ca viyadādinānāvidhamalarūpapariṇāmāspadaṃ ceti pratyavatiṣṭhante.
ramanuja/vedartha-samgraha/§9.english_close: Others again, while describing reality through cognition of identity, maintain that Brahman itself, an ocean of unsurpassed and measureless exalted qualities, is by nature both different and non-different and is the locus of manifold transformations such as space and the rest.

ramanuja/vedartha-samgraha/§15.sanskrit_iast: tajjñānenāsya nikhilasya jñātatāṃ bruvaṃl lokadṛṣṭaṃ kāryakāraṇayor ananyatvaṃ darśayituṃ dṛṣṭāntam āha -- yathā somyaikena mṛtpiṇḍena sarvaṃ mṛnmayaṃ vijñātaṃ syād vācārambhaṇaṃ vikāro nāmadheyaṃ mṛttikety eva satyam iti.
ramanuja/vedartha-samgraha/§15.english_close: To show that by knowing that one all this is known, scripture gives the familiar example of non-difference of effect and cause: by one lump of clay all things made of clay are known; the modification is only a name arising in speech, while clay alone is the reality.

ramanuja/vedartha-samgraha/§16.sanskrit_iast: sa hovāca sad eva somyedam agra āsīd ekam evādvitīyam iti ... tasmin kāle jagataḥ sadātmakatāṃ sad eveti pratipādya ... sadāpannasya jagatas tadānīm avibhaktanāmarūpatāṃ pratipādya tatpratipādanenaiva sato jagadupādānatvaṃ pratipāditam.
ramanuja/vedartha-samgraha/§16.english_close: ‘Being alone, one without a second, was this in the beginning’: this teaches that the world was of the nature of sat before creation, with name and form then undivided, and thereby establishes that sat is the material cause of the world.

ramanuja/vedartha-samgraha/§18.sanskrit_iast: evaṃ samastacidacidātmakaprapañcasya sadupādānatāsannimittatāsadādhāratāsanniyamyatāsaccheṣatādi sarvaṃ ca ... aitadātmyam idaṃ sarvaṃ tatsatyam iti kṛtsnasya jagato brahmātmakatvam eva satyam iti pratipādya kṛtsnasya jagataḥ sa evātmā kṛtsnaṃ jagat tasya śarīram.
ramanuja/vedartha-samgraha/§18.english_close: Thus, after teaching the whole cit-acit cosmos as having Sat as material cause, efficient cause, support, controller, and end, scripture declares that the entire world has Brahman as its self and that the entire world is his body.

annambhatta/tarka-sangraha/38.english_close: A cause is that which antecedently exists with respect to a determinate effect.

annambhatta/tarka-sangraha/39.english_close: An effect is the counterpositive of prior non-existence.

annambhatta/tarka-sangraha/40.english_close: Cause is threefold, by distinction of samavāyi, asamavāyi, and nimitta.

annambhatta/tarka-sangraha/80.sanskrit_iast: anādiḥ sāntaḥ prāgabhāvaḥ / utpatteḥ pūrvaṃ kāryasya.
annambhatta/tarka-sangraha/80.english_close: Prior non-existence is beginningless but has an end; it is the state of the effect before its production.

umasvati/tattvartha-sutra/sat-dravya-lak.english_close: The mark of real substance is this: the real is endowed with origination, decay, and permanence.

umasvati/tattvartha-sutra/guna-paryayava.english_close: Substance is that which possesses qualities and modes.

aksapada/nyaya-sutra/4.1.19.english_close: Īśvara is the cause, since one observes that human action is fruitless without him. (siddhānta-sūtra)

aksapada/nyaya-sutra/4.1.20.english_close: No, because in the absence of human action no fruit arises. (pūrvapakṣa-sūtra)

aksapada/nyaya-sutra/4.1.21.english_close: Since human action is itself caused by him, it does not constitute a counter-cause. (siddhānta-sūtra)

nagarjuna/mula-madhyamaka-karika/24.40.sanskrit_iast: yaḥ pratītyasamutpādaṃ paśyatīdaṃ sa paśyati / duḥkhaṃ samudayaṃ caiva nirodhaṃ mārgam eva ca //
nagarjuna/mula-madhyamaka-karika/24.40.english_close: He who sees this dependent arising sees suffering, its arising, its cessation, and the path.
